message: fix: update @moltbot → @openclaw and Moltbot → OpenClaw in testimonials

files.patch:
@@ -2,21 +2,21 @@
   {
     "id": "2012535486401671588",
     "author": "dreetje",
-    "quote": "What I'm currently doing with @moltbot:
• Check my incoming mail, and remove spam
• Check my incoming messages (through Beeper)
• Order things for me
• Send my reminders to Tana
• Create issues on GitHub
• Sync my Google Places
• Reads my X bookmarks and discusses them with me
• Generate a nice pdf summary of car conversations
• Keep track of costs and split them after trips
• Impersonate me in a group chat with friends (Hilarious)
• It can call me and we can chat
• Has its own 1Password vault it can read and write to

And the beauty is, IT built all of this, just by chatting to it on the phone.",
+    "quote": "What I'm currently doing with @openclaw:
• Check my incoming mail, and remove spam
• Check my incoming messages (through Beeper)
• Order things for me
• Send my reminders to Tana
• Create issues on GitHub
• Sync my Google Places
• Reads my X bookmarks and discusses them with me
• Generate a nice pdf summary of car conversations
• Keep track of costs and split them after trips
• Impersonate me in a group chat with friends (Hilarious)
• It can call me and we can chat
• Has its own 1Password vault it can read and write to

And the beauty is, IT built all of this, just by chatting to it on the phone.",
     "category": "automation",
     "likes": 271
   },
   {
     "id": "2012565160586625345",
     "author": "danpeguine",
-    "quote": "Things my @moltbot does for me:
• Timeblocks tasks in my calendar based on importance
• Scores tasks importance and urgency based on an algorithm we're developing together
• Leads me through a weekly review based on meeting transcriptions & notes
• Gives a morning daily brief: weather, weekly objectives, health stats, meetings agenda, key reminders, trending topics, stuff I should read based on current objectives, a relevant quote from my books
• Notifies my wife and I about our son's upcoming school tests
• Researches big projects and breaks them down to tasks
• Researches people before meetings and creates briefing docs
• Spawns background sub-agents to research business ideas
• Manages the calendar for any conflicts autonomously
• Creates invoices and summarizes work beautifully",
+    "quote": "Things my @openclaw does for me:
• Timeblocks tasks in my calendar based on importance
• Scores tasks importance and urgency based on an algorithm we're developing together
• Leads me through a weekly review based on meeting transcriptions & notes
• Gives a morning daily brief: weather, weekly objectives, health stats, meetings agenda, key reminders, trending topics, stuff I should read based on current objectives, a relevant quote from my books
• Notifies my wife and I about our son's upcoming school tests
• Researches big projects and breaks them down to tasks
• Researches people before meetings and creates briefing docs
• Spawns background sub-agents to research business ideas
• Manages the calendar for any conflicts autonomously
• Creates invoices and summarizes work beautifully",
     "category": "productivity",
     "likes": 257
   },
   {
     "id": "2009487467817336895",
     "author": "davis7",
-    "quote": "This is the "blue text bubbles" moment of the ai race

All the cool shit u've been seeing about building crazy custom agents on top of a coding agent?

moltbot, btca, and the thousands more that will get built this year now HAVE to be built using anthropic's "agent sdk" package or else they'll be 100x more expensive

and if they're built on anthropic's sdk guess what they can't do? switch to a better model from another provider when it releases",
+    "quote": "This is the "blue text bubbles" moment of the ai race

All the cool shit u've been seeing about building crazy custom agents on top of a coding agent?

OpenClaw, btca, and the thousands more that will get built this year now HAVE to be built using anthropic's "agent sdk" package or else they'll be 100x more expensive

and if they're built on anthropic's sdk guess what they can't do? switch to a better model from another provider when it releases",

     "likes": 229,

@@ -132,14 +132,14 @@
   {
     "id": "2012119327147798753",
     "author": "georgedagg_",
-    "quote": "I had a holy shit, the world is not the same anymore moment last night.

Put BigC, my @moltbot on a hetzner server and my mind is just blown.

We all know that AI is transformative but I don't think you really feel it until you step outside the prompt/response loop and step into the prompt/take real actions on my behalf/response loop.

- I got BigC to check on the deployment of a new railway project
- Builds for each of the services in the project failed
- BigC reviewed the logs
- Then reviewed the project on the server
- Identified the root cause (incorrect build commands on each service)
- Updated the configs of each in railway
- Redeployed
- Confirmed everything worked
- I then tested it
- Identified a design issue
- Got BigC to review, fix and submit a PR
- job done

oh and all this was done over voice when walking the fucking dog...",
+    "quote": "I had a holy shit, the world is not the same anymore moment last night.

Put BigC, my @openclaw on a hetzner server and my mind is just blown.

We all know that AI is transformative but I don't think you really feel it until you step outside the prompt/response loop and step into the prompt/take real actions on my behalf/response loop.

- I got BigC to check on the deployment of a new railway project
- Builds for each of the services in the project failed
- BigC reviewed the logs
- Then reviewed the project on the server
- Identified the root cause (incorrect build commands on each service)
- Updated the configs of each in railway
- Redeployed
- Confirmed everything worked
- I then tested it
- Identified a design issue
- Got BigC to review, fix and submit a PR
- job done

oh and all this was done over voice when walking the fucking dog...",
     "category": "developer",
     "likes": 143
   },

     "id": "2007616854689280196",
     "author": "stevecaldwell",
-    "quote": "Update on Crawdad 🦞 (my AI Assistant running on @moltbot by @steipete)

Tonight we built a full weekly meal planning system in Notion.

My wife and I take turns cooking, so we had Crawdad set up:

- Master meal plan template for all of 2026 (365 days)
- Shopping lists sorted by store and aisle (Kroger/Costco/Lowes)
- Weather forecast auto-updating on the meal plan list (helps me plan good nights for grilling, soup, etc)
- Our go-to recipes catalogued by chef

- It'll remind me in morning/evening digests if I need to plan dinner or grab groceries.

I imagine that this will save us at least an hour a week.",
+    "quote": "Update on Crawdad 🦞 (my AI Assistant running on @openclaw by @steipete)

Tonight we built a full weekly meal planning system in Notion.

My wife and I take turns cooking, so we had Crawdad set up:

- Master meal plan template for all of 2026 (365 days)
- Shopping lists sorted by store and aisle (Kroger/Costco/Lowes)
- Weather forecast auto-updating on the meal plan list (helps me plan good nights for grilling, soup, etc)
- Our go-to recipes catalogued by chef

- It'll remind me in morning/evening digests if I need to plan dinner or grab groceries.

I imagine that this will save us at least an hour a week.",
     "category": "family",
     "likes": 133,
     "images": [

   {
     "id": "2011260468975771862",
     "author": "iamtrebuh",
-    "quote": "My AI setup as a solo founder

built it with @moltbot, here's how it works:

4 agents, each with their own job:

- main one: strategy, planning, big picture. coordinates the others
- dev agent: coding, technical problems, architecture decisions
- marketing agent: research, content ideas, competitor analysis
- business agent: pricing, metrics, growth strategy

what makes it actually useful:

- shared memory for the big stuff (project docs, goals, key decisions)
- but each agent also has their own context (past messages)
- different models for different jobs (codex for coding, Gemini for marketing etc)
- scheduled daily tasks without me asking (content prompts, reddit monitoring, reminders)
- agents can work in parallel
- connected to my notes so nothing gets lost

It all runs on my VPS and I control them all through Telegram, so it basically works as a real small team available 24/7. I even gave them names and different characters:
- Milo (leader - confident and charismatic),
- Josh (business guy - pragmatic and straight to the point, numbers-driven),
- Angela (marketing - extroverted, funny and full of ideas),
- Bob (coding - introverted analytical genius).

I'm still setting up all the skills, but so far it really works great and it's a first use case that's so beneficial to me and can help me in my daily work.",
+    "quote": "My AI setup as a solo founder

built it with @openclaw, here's how it works:

4 agents, each with their own job:

- main one: strategy, planning, big picture. coordinates the others
- dev agent: coding, technical problems, architecture decisions
- marketing agent: research, content ideas, competitor analysis
- business agent: pricing, metrics, growth strategy

what makes it actually useful:

- shared memory for the big stuff (project docs, goals, key decisions)
- but each agent also has their own context (past messages)
- different models for different jobs (codex for coding, Gemini for marketing etc)
- scheduled daily tasks without me asking (content prompts, reddit monitoring, reminders)
- agents can work in parallel
- connected to my notes so nothing gets lost

It all runs on my VPS and I control them all through Telegram, so it basically works as a real small team available 24/7. I even gave them names and different characters:
- Milo (leader - confident and charismatic),
- Josh (business guy - pragmatic and straight to the point, numbers-driven),
- Angela (marketing - extroverted, funny and full of ideas),
- Bob (coding - introverted analytical genius).

I'm still setting up all the skills, but so far it really works great and it's a first use case that's so beneficial to me and can help me in my daily work.",
     "category": "developer",
     "likes": 11,
     "images": [
@@ -218,7 +218,7 @@
   {
     "id": "2012397507754770587",
     "author": "antonplex",
-    "quote": "I've been experimenting with a new system to keep my @moltbot busy:
> I created a "log" skill that captures my thoughts as they move through the idea-to-decision pipeline.

> Throughout the day, I send any interesting ideas, and it records each as a TASK.

> Overnight, a cron job picks up a task and spawns experiments (exploration, research via scientist subagents, writing code, etc.).

> The next morning, I review the results and either assign follow-up tasks or make a decision.

> Each decision is captured as a decision record, similar to an ADR in software, including problem context, considered alternatives, pros and cons, and the final proposed solution.

> All these artifacts live in a log directory with templates and file naming conventions, enabling the agent to write complex search expressions.",
+    "quote": "I've been experimenting with a new system to keep my @openclaw busy:
> I created a "log" skill that captures my thoughts as they move through the idea-to-decision pipeline.

> Throughout the day, I send any interesting ideas, and it records each as a TASK.

> Overnight, a cron job picks up a task and spawns experiments (exploration, research via scientist subagents, writing code, etc.).

> The next morning, I review the results and either assign follow-up tasks or make a decision.

> Each decision is captured as a decision record, similar to an ADR in software, including problem context, considered alternatives, pros and cons, and the final proposed solution.

> All these artifacts live in a log directory with templates and file naming conventions, enabling the agent to write complex search expressions.",

     "likes": 4,

-    "quote": "My new favorite way to work with Moltbot:

1. Tell it we're working on app improvements, it should just take notes
2. Run through user flows, send screenshots with notes as I find issues
3. It builds a list of work, adds its own hypotheses
4. Writes up a full report on what to do
5. I say "Go" and it kicks off Codex agents to attack the different pieces, opening separate PRs
6. Claude reviews & improves the PRs on Github
7. Claw makes a doc of everything we did + what to test
8. Next time I test, I start with that list to make sure everything worked",

4. Writes up a full report on what to do
5. I say "Go" and it kicks off Codex agents to attack the different pieces, opening separate PRs
6. Claude reviews & improves the PRs on Github
7. Claw makes a doc of everything we did + what to test
